question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TopicController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of topicService.findById() followed by entityService.findById() to cause a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) inconsistency where a topic is deleted between the two calls, resulting in a partial response that misrepresents the gossip transaction topic's state?
